events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Richard Starkey, known as Ringo Starr, was born on July 7, 1940, and rose to fame as the drummer for The Beatles.

angles.name: Ringo's Beat
angles.copy: Happy 86th Birthday to the legendary Ringo Starr! From the Fab Four to his All-Starr Band, his rhythm is unmistakable. What's your favourite Ringo track?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Brian May, born on July 19, 1947, is the iconic guitarist and songwriter for the rock band Queen.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Sending a huge 79th birthday shout-out to the phenomenal Brian May! His guitar riffs with Queen are pure magic. What's the Queen song that always gets you?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, born on July 26, 1943, is the charismatic lead singer and a founding member of The Rolling Stones.

angles.name: Rock & Roll Icon
angles.copy: Happy 83rd Birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger! The energy he brings to the stage is unmatched. What's your top Rolling Stones anthem?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: MTV, the first 24-hour music video channel, launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its inaugural video.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched and changed music forever, kicking off with The Buggles' iconic 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. Remember the first time you saw a music video?

events.title: Oasis' Definitely Maybe released (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which became a cornerstone of the Britpop era.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed the sound of British music. Which track from their debut still gives you chills?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, an iconic event of the 1960s counterculture, took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: 57 years ago, half a million people gathered for '3 Days of Peace & Music' at Woodstock. A truly defining moment in music history. Which Woodstock performance is etched in your memory?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death (1977)
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King of Rock & Roll, Elvis Presley, who left us on this day in 1977. His legacy continues to inspire generations. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death (1996)
events.blurb: Tupac Shakur, an influential figure in hip-hop, was tragically shot in Las Vegas on September 7, 1996, and died on September 13, 1996.

angles.name: Remembering Tupac
angles.copy: Remembering the profound impact of Tupac Shakur, who was tragically shot on this day in 1996. His music and message continue to resonate.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death (1970)
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, widely regarded as one of the greatest guitarists of all time, died on September 18, 1970, at the age of 27.

angles.name: Guitar Legend Remembered
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled talent of Jimi Hendrix, who passed away on this day in 1970. His revolutionary guitar work continues to inspire.

events.title: The Beatles' Abbey Road released (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic album 'Abbey Road' was released in the UK on September 26, 1969, and in the US on October 1, 1969.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: 57 years since The Beatles gave us the masterpiece that is 'Abbey Road'. That album cover alone is iconic! What's your favourite track from it?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, born on September 7, 1951, is the lead vocalist, guitarist, and primary songwriter of The Pretenders.

angles.name: Pretenders Frontwoman
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! Her distinctive voice and songwriting with The Pretenders are legendary. What's your go-to Pretenders track?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, born on September 21, 1972, is best known as the lead singer of Oasis and later Beady Eye.

angles.name: Mad Fer It!
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! From Oasis anthems to his solo hits, he's still got that swagger. What's your favourite Liam moment?

events.title: John Lennon's Imagine released (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic song 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on October 11, 1971, and was the title track of his album released on September 9, 1971.

angles.name: Imagine The Song
angles.copy: 55 years since John Lennon released 'Imagine', a song that continues to inspire peace and unity worldwide. A timeless message.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple introduced the first-generation iPod, revolutionising portable music, on October 23, 2001, and it began shipping on November 10, 2001.

angles.name: The Original iPod
angles.copy: Remember when the first iPod dropped? 25 years ago, Apple changed how we listen to music forever. What was the first album you loaded onto yours?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day is celebrated annually on October 5th, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Bond, James Bond
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating 64 years of 007's cinematic adventures. Who's your favourite Bond and what's the ultimate Bond theme tune?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Lennon, born on October 9, 1940, was a co-founder of The Beatles and a prolific solo artist.

angles.name: Remembering John Lennon
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the visionary John Lennon. His music and message of peace continue to inspire.
